老師要求學生用正確的科學術語清楚地標示他們的圖表，以準備即將到來的生物考試。
動詞「貼標籤/標示」。
It's unfair to label someone as 'lazy' without understanding their circumstances; everyone faces different challenges.
在不了解對方情況下就給人貼上「懶惰」的標籤是不公平的；每個人都面臨不同的挑戰。
動詞「歸類/貼標籤」。常用搭配：label sb as sth。
Related: labeled, labelled, labeling, labelling

名詞「鞋帶/蕾絲」或動詞「繫鞋帶」。動詞三態為 lace-laced-laced。
He tripped and fell because his shoelace came undone; he should have tied his lace more tightly before running.
他因為鞋帶鬆了而絆倒，他應該在跑步前把鞋帶繫得更緊。
名詞「鞋帶」。常用搭配：tie one's laces (繫鞋帶)。
The bride's wedding dress was beautifully decorated with delicate white lace, adding an elegant touch to her special day.
新娘的婚紗用精緻的白色蕾絲裝飾得非常漂亮，為她的特殊日子增添了優雅的氣息。
名詞「蕾絲」。
Before starting the marathon, athletes carefully lace up their running shoes to ensure a secure and comfortable fit.
在開始馬拉松之前，運動員會仔細地繫好他們的跑鞋，以確保牢固舒適的貼合。
動詞「繫鞋帶」。常用片語：lace up (繫緊)。
Related: laced

名詞「梯子」。常用於攀爬高處。片語 "climb the ladder" 可指「升職」。
The firefighter used a long ladder to reach the window of the burning building and rescue the trapped residents inside.
消防員使用長梯到達燃燒建築物的窗戶，並救出被困在裡面的居民。
名詞「梯子」。
Many young professionals aspire to climb the corporate ladder quickly, hoping to achieve success and higher positions.
許多年輕專業人士渴望快速攀登企業階梯，希望能取得成功和更高的職位。
片語 climb the corporate ladder (攀登企業階梯/升職)。

副詞「最近」。常與現在完成式或現在完成進行式連用。注意與 late (遲到) 的區別。
I haven't seen much of my best friend lately; she has been really busy preparing for her university entrance exams.
我最近很少見到我最好的朋友；她一直忙著準備大學入學考試。
副詞「最近」。常與現在完成式連用。

名詞「笑聲」。不可數名詞。動詞為 laugh。
The sound of children's joyful laughter filled the park as they played happily on the swings and slides.
孩子們歡快的笑聲充滿了公園，他們在鞦韆和滑梯上快樂地玩耍。
名詞「笑聲」。常用搭配：fill with laughter (充滿笑聲)。
Related: laugh

名詞「待洗衣物/洗好的衣物/洗衣店」。不可數名詞。
After a week-long camping trip, we had a huge pile of dirty laundry that needed to be washed immediately.
經過一週的露營旅行後，我們有一大堆髒衣服需要立即清洗。
名詞「待洗衣物」。常用搭配：do the laundry (洗衣服)。
My mother usually does the laundry every Saturday morning, making sure all our clothes are clean and fresh.
我媽媽通常每個星期六早上洗衣服，確保我們所有的衣服都乾淨清新。
名詞「洗衣服這件事」。do the laundry 是固定片語。

名詞「草坪」。常用於房屋周圍或公共場所的草地。
The school gardener mows the lawn every week to keep the campus looking neat and tidy for students and visitors.
學校園丁每週修剪草坪，以保持校園對學生和訪客來說整潔。
名詞「草坪」。常用搭配：mow the lawn (割草坪)。

名詞「洩漏、漏洞」或「消息走漏」。動詞「洩漏（液體、氣體、秘密等）」。動詞常搭配介係詞 out，表示「洩漏出去」。
The old pipe had a small leak, causing water to drip constantly and damage the ceiling over time.
那根舊水管有個小漏洞，導致水不斷滴落，久而久之損壞了天花板。
名詞「漏洞、裂縫」。指液體或氣體逸出的孔洞。
The sudden resignation of the CEO was due to a significant data leak that exposed sensitive company information.
執行長突然辭職是因為一次重大的資料洩漏，該事件暴露了公司的敏感資訊。
名詞「洩漏（消息、秘密）」。指秘密資訊被揭露。
The gas tank started to leak after the accident, posing a serious safety risk to everyone nearby.
事故後，油箱開始洩漏，對附近所有人造成了嚴重的安全風險。
動詞「洩漏（液體、氣體）」。指液體或氣體從容器中逸出。
Despite strict security measures, the confidential report somehow leaked out to the public before its official release.
儘管有嚴格的安全措施，這份機密報告不知何故在正式發布前洩漏給了公眾。
動詞「洩漏（秘密、消息）」。常搭配介係詞 out，表示消息或秘密被公開。
Related: leaks, leaked, leaking

動詞「跳躍、跳過」。名詞「跳躍」或「巨大的進步」。片語 take a leap of faith (放手一搏、大膽嘗試) 是常見考點。
The agile deer leaped over the fallen log effortlessly, disappearing into the dense forest in seconds.
這隻敏捷的鹿輕鬆地跳過了倒下的圓木，幾秒鐘內消失在茂密的森林中。
動詞「跳躍、跳過」。指快速或突然地跳起來或跳過某物。
With a powerful leap, the athlete cleared the high bar, securing a new personal best in the competition.
運動員以一個有力的跳躍，越過了高竿，在比賽中創下了新的個人最佳成績。
名詞「跳躍」。指跳起來的動作。
The company made a significant leap in technology, developing a new product that revolutionized the industry.
該公司在技術上取得了重大飛躍，開發出一款徹底改變了行業的新產品。
名詞「巨大的進步、飛躍」。常用搭配：a leap in technology/progress。
Despite the risks, she decided to take a leap of faith and start her own business, hoping for success.
儘管有風險，她還是決定放手一搏，創辦自己的事業，希望能成功。
片語 take a leap of faith「放手一搏、大膽嘗試」。指在沒有完全確定的情況下，憑信心去做某事。
Related: leaps, leaped, leapt, leaping

名詞「學習、學問」。為不可數名詞。常用於描述學習的過程或所獲得的知識。
Effective learning strategies are crucial for students to absorb complex information and perform well in exams.
有效的學習策略對於學生吸收複雜資訊並在考試中取得好成績至關重要。
名詞「學習」。指獲取知識或技能的過程。
Throughout his life, the professor dedicated himself to the pursuit of learning, constantly reading and researching new topics.
這位教授一生致力於追求學問，不斷閱讀和研究新主題。
名詞「學問、知識」。指透過學習所獲得的知識或學識。
The internship provided valuable learning experiences, allowing students to apply theoretical knowledge in a real-world setting.
這次實習提供了寶貴的學習經驗，讓學生能夠將理論知識應用於實際環境中。
名詞「學習經驗」。指透過實踐或經歷所獲得的知識或技能。
Related: learn

名詞「皮革」。為不可數名詞，指由動物皮製成的材料。常用於描述物品的材質。
Many high-quality bags and shoes are made from leather because of its durability and classic appearance.
許多高品質的包包和鞋子都是用皮革製成的，因為它耐用且外觀經典。
名詞「皮革」。指作為材料的皮革，不可數。
The old armchair, upholstered in soft leather, was the most comfortable spot in the entire living room.
那張用柔軟皮革裝飾的舊扶手椅，是整個客廳裡最舒適的地方。
名詞「皮革」。指作為材料的皮革，常用介係詞 in 或 from。

名詞「閒暇、空閒」。為不可數名詞。常用片語 at leisure (有空、從容地), leisure time (閒暇時間), leisure activities (休閒活動)。
During her leisure time, she enjoys reading novels and painting watercolors, finding these activities very relaxing.
在她的閒暇時間，她喜歡閱讀小說和畫水彩畫，覺得這些活動非常放鬆。
名詞「閒暇」。常用搭配 leisure time。
The resort offers a wide range of leisure activities, from swimming and tennis to spa treatments, for guests to enjoy.
這家度假村提供各式各樣的休閒活動，從游泳、網球到水療，供客人享受。
名詞「休閒」。常用搭配 leisure activities。
Students often look forward to the summer break, when they can pursue their hobbies at leisure without academic pressure.
學生們通常期待暑假，屆時他們可以從容地追求自己的愛好，而沒有學業壓力。
片語 at leisure「從容地、有空」。指有時間做某事，不匆忙。

名詞「檸檬水」。可數或不可數。指一種由檸檬汁、糖和水製成的飲料。
On a hot summer day, nothing is more refreshing than a tall glass of cold lemonade with ice.
在炎熱的夏日，沒有什麼比一杯加冰的冰涼檸檬水更提神了。
名詞「檸檬水」。指一種飲料，此處為不可數用法。
The children set up a small stand to sell homemade lemonade to raise money for their school trip.
孩子們搭起一個小攤位賣自製檸檬水，為他們的學校旅行籌錢。
名詞「檸檬水」。指一種飲料，此處為不可數用法。
Related: lemonades

名詞「豹」。指一種大型貓科動物，以其身上獨特的斑點而聞名。
The elusive leopard is a master of camouflage, often blending seamlessly into its natural habitat in the jungle.
這隻難以捉摸的豹是偽裝大師，經常完美地融入其在叢林中的自然棲息地。
名詞「豹」。指一種大型貓科動物。
In some cultures, the strength and agility of the leopard are admired, symbolizing power and grace.
在某些文化中，豹的力量和敏捷性受到推崇，象徵著權力和優雅。
名詞「豹」。指一種動物，作為力量和優雅的象徵。
Related: leopards

名詞「萵苣」。為不可數名詞。指一種常見的綠葉蔬菜，常用於沙拉和三明治。
For a healthy lunch, she often prepares a fresh salad with crisp lettuce, tomatoes, and a light vinaigrette dressing.
為了健康的午餐，她經常準備一份新鮮沙拉，裡面有脆生菜、番茄和清淡的油醋醬。
名詞「萵苣」。指一種蔬菜，通常為不可數名詞。
The farmer carefully harvested the rows of vibrant green lettuce, ensuring each head was perfect for market.
農夫小心翼翼地收穫了一排排鮮綠的萵苣，確保每一顆都完美無缺，可以上市。
名詞「萵苣」。指一種蔬菜，作為農產品。

名詞「自由」。為不可數名詞。指免於束縛或限制的狀態。常用片語 at liberty (自由的、有空的), take liberties (冒犯、隨意)。
The Statue of Liberty in New York Harbor symbolizes freedom and democracy for many immigrants arriving in the United States.
紐約港的自由女神像對許多抵達美國的移民來說，象徵著自由和民主。
名詞「自由」。指免於壓迫或限制的狀態，此處為專有名詞的一部分。
After years of oppression, the people finally fought for their liberty and established an independent nation.
經過多年的壓迫，人民最終為他們的自由而戰，並建立了一個獨立的國家。
名詞「自由」。指個人或國家免於控制或奴役的權利。
The teacher told the students they were at liberty to choose any topic for their research paper, encouraging creativity.
老師告訴學生，他們可以自由選擇研究報告的任何主題，鼓勵他們發揮創意。
片語 at liberty to do sth「自由做某事」。指有權利或被允許做某事。
Related: liberties

動詞「舔」。名詞「舔的動作」。動詞指用舌頭觸碰或移動。
The dog joyfully licked its owner's face to show affection after he returned home from work.
狗在主人下班回家後，高興地舔著他的臉以示親熱。
動詞「舔」。指用舌頭觸碰或移動。
Children often love to lick the ice cream cone slowly to savor every bit of the sweet treat.
孩子們通常喜歡慢慢舔冰淇淋筒，以品嚐這甜點的每一點滋味。
動詞「舔」。指用舌頭舔食。
With a quick lick, the cat cleaned its paw before continuing its nap in the warm sunlight.
貓快速地舔了一下爪子，然後繼續在溫暖的陽光下打盹。
名詞「舔的動作」。指用舌頭快速觸碰的動作。
Related: licks, licked, licking

名詞「一生、壽命」。常用片語 in one's lifetime (在某人一生中)。
Dr. Lee dedicated his entire lifetime to scientific research, making significant contributions to the field of medicine.
李博士將他的一生奉獻給科學研究，對醫學領域做出了重大貢獻。
名詞「一生、壽命」。常用搭配：dedicate one's lifetime to (將一生奉獻給...)。
Receiving this prestigious award is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity for the young artist to gain international recognition.
獲得這個享有盛譽的獎項對這位年輕藝術家來說是千載難逢的機會，能獲得國際認可。
片語 once-in-a-lifetime (一生一次的、千載難逢的)。

名詞「燈塔」。通常指引船隻方向，避免危險。
The ancient lighthouse stood tall on the cliff, guiding ships safely through the treacherous waters during stormy nights.
古老的燈塔高聳於懸崖上，在暴風雨的夜晚引導船隻安全穿越險惡的水域。
名詞「燈塔」。常用動詞搭配：stand tall (高聳)、guide ships (引導船隻)。
Volunteers worked tirelessly to restore the historic lighthouse, ensuring its light would continue to protect sailors for generations.
志工們不懈努力修復這座歷史悠久的燈塔，確保它的光芒能繼續保護水手們世代相傳。
名詞「燈塔」。常用搭配：restore a lighthouse (修復燈塔)、historic lighthouse (歷史悠久的燈塔)。

名詞「閃電」。不可數。常用片語 as quick as lightning (快如閃電)。注意與 thunder (雷聲) 的區別。
During the severe thunderstorm, a bright flash of lightning illuminated the dark sky, followed by a loud clap of thunder.
在劇烈的雷暴期間，一道明亮的閃電照亮了黑暗的天空，隨後傳來一聲響亮的雷鳴。
名詞「閃電」。不可數。常用搭配：flash of lightning (一道閃電)。
The athlete's reaction time was as quick as lightning, allowing him to catch the ball effortlessly and win the game.
這位運動員的反應速度快如閃電，讓他毫不費力地接住球並贏得比賽。
片語 as quick as lightning (快如閃電)。

名詞「百合花」。常用於象徵純潔。
The bride carried a beautiful bouquet of white lilies, symbolizing purity and new beginnings on her wedding day.
新娘手捧一束美麗的白色百合花，象徵著婚禮當天的純潔和新開始。
名詞「百合花」。常用搭配：white lilies (白色百合)、bouquet of lilies (一束百合)。
In many cultures, the lily is considered a sacred flower, often used in religious ceremonies and traditional decorations.
在許多文化中，百合被視為一種神聖的花朵，常用於宗教儀式和傳統裝飾。
名詞「百合花」。

名詞「肢體；樹枝」。常用片語 out on a limb (處於危險/不利的境地)。
After the accident, the doctor carefully examined the patient's injured limb to determine the extent of the damage.
事故發生後，醫生仔細檢查了病患受傷的肢體，以確定受損程度。
名詞「肢體」。指手臂或腿。
The adventurous cat climbed out on a limb of the tall oak tree, looking for a bird's nest.
這隻愛冒險的貓爬到高大的橡樹枝上，尋找鳥巢。
名詞「樹枝」。片語 out on a limb (處於危險/不利的境地)。

名詞「垃圾；一窩幼崽」。不可數。動詞「亂丟垃圾」。注意名詞與動詞的詞性變化。
Students are encouraged to dispose of their litter properly in the designated bins to keep the campus clean.
鼓勵學生將垃圾妥善丟棄在指定的垃圾桶中，以保持校園清潔。
名詞「垃圾」。不可數。常用搭配：dispose of litter (處理垃圾)。
It is illegal to litter in public parks, and offenders may face heavy fines for polluting the environment.
在公園亂丟垃圾是違法的，違規者可能會因污染環境而面臨巨額罰款。
動詞「亂丟垃圾」。
The stray dog gave birth to a litter of five adorable puppies behind the old abandoned building.
那隻流浪狗在廢棄的舊建築後面生了一窩五隻可愛的小狗。
名詞「一窩幼崽」。常用搭配：a litter of puppies/kittens (一窩小狗/小貓)。

形容詞「活潑的；生動的」。注意字尾 -ly 卻是形容詞，而非副詞。
The teacher encouraged the students to have a lively discussion about the novel, sharing their different interpretations.
老師鼓勵學生們對這部小說進行熱烈的討論，分享他們不同的解讀。
形容詞「活潑的；熱烈的」。常用搭配：lively discussion (熱烈討論)。
Despite her old age, Grandma still has a very lively spirit and enjoys participating in various community activities.
儘管年事已高，奶奶仍然精神奕奕，喜歡參與各種社區活動。
形容詞「活潑的；精神奕奕的」。常用搭配：lively spirit (活潑的精神)。
Related: liveliness

名詞「一條麵包」。複數 loaves。常用搭配 a loaf of bread。
She bought a fresh loaf of whole wheat bread from the bakery to make sandwiches for lunch.
她從麵包店買了一條新鮮的全麥麵包，準備做午餐三明治。
名詞「一條麵包」。常用搭配：a loaf of bread (一條麵包)。注意複數 loaves。
The baker carefully sliced the warm loaf into thick pieces, ready to be served with butter and jam.
麵包師小心翼翼地將溫熱的麵包切成厚片，準備搭配奶油和果醬上桌。
名詞「一條麵包」。
Related: loaves

名詞「大廳；遊說團體」。
The hotel lobby was beautifully decorated for the holidays, with a large Christmas tree and festive lights.
飯店大廳為節日裝飾得美輪美奐，擺放著一棵巨大的聖誕樹和節日燈飾。
名詞「大廳」。常用搭配：hotel lobby (飯店大廳)。
An environmental lobby group is trying to influence policymakers to adopt stricter regulations on pollution.
一個環保遊說團體正試圖影響決策者，以通過更嚴格的污染法規。
名詞「遊說團體」。常用搭配：lobby group (遊說團體)。

動詞「找出；位於」。常用被動語態 be located in/at/on (位於)。
After hours of searching, the rescue team finally located the missing hiker deep in the dense forest.
經過數小時的搜尋，搜救隊終於在茂密的森林深處找到了失蹤的登山客。
動詞「找出；確定...的位置」。
The new university campus will be located on the outskirts of the city, offering students a peaceful learning environment.
新大學校區將位於城市的郊區，為學生提供一個寧靜的學習環境。
動詞「位於」。常用被動語態 be located in/at/on。此處用 on。
Scientists are working to locate the source of the mysterious signal coming from deep space.
科學家們正在努力定位來自深空的神秘信號源。
動詞「定位；找出」。
Related: location, localized

名詞「地點、位置」。常用片語 in a good/bad location (地理位置好/不好)；on location (實地拍攝)。注意介係詞搭配。
The new library's location near the university campus makes it easily accessible for students and faculty members.
新圖書館位於大學校園附近的地點，讓學生和教職員工都能輕鬆前往。
名詞「地點、位置」。常搭配介係詞 near, at, in。
For the documentary, the film crew decided to shoot on location in the Amazon rainforest to capture authentic wildlife footage.
為了這部紀錄片，電影攝製組決定在亞馬遜雨林實地拍攝，以捕捉真實的野生動物畫面。
片語 on location 表「實地拍攝/在現場」。
Related: locate, localized

動詞「鎖上」；名詞「鎖」。動詞片語 lock up (鎖起來)；lock out (反鎖在外)。名詞片語 under lock and key (妥善保管)。
Before leaving for the long holiday, she always remembers to lock all the doors and windows to ensure her apartment's safety.
在去長假之前，她總是記得鎖上所有的門窗，以確保公寓的安全。
動詞「鎖上」。常用搭配 lock the door/window。
The old wooden chest had a rusty lock that made it difficult to open, hinting at forgotten treasures inside.
這個舊木箱上有一把生鏽的鎖，很難打開，暗示著裡面有被遺忘的寶藏。
名詞「鎖」。
The mischievous children accidentally locked their parents out of the house, causing panic before they found the spare key.
調皮的孩子們不小心把父母反鎖在屋外，在他們找到備用鑰匙之前引起了一陣恐慌。
動詞片語 lock out 表「反鎖在外」。
Related: locked, unlock

名詞「原木、日誌」；動詞「記錄、伐木」。常用片語 log in/on (登入)；log out/off (登出)。
The lumberjacks carefully stacked the freshly cut logs by the river, preparing them for transport to the sawmill.
伐木工人小心翼翼地將剛砍下的原木堆放在河邊，準備運往鋸木廠。
名詞「原木」。
Students must log in to the school's online learning platform using their unique ID and password to access course materials.
學生必須使用他們獨特的帳號和密碼登入學校的線上學習平台，才能存取課程資料。
動詞片語 log in/on 表「登入」。
The captain meticulously logged every significant event of the voyage, including weather changes, in the ship's journal.
船長在航海日誌中詳細記錄了航程中的每一個重要事件，包括天氣變化。
動詞「記錄」。
Related: logger, logging

名詞「棒棒糖」。此字較生活化，GSAT較少考，但仍可能出現在閱讀測驗中。
The little girl happily licked her strawberry-flavored lollipop while watching the parade, her face covered in a sticky smile.
小女孩一邊看遊行，一邊開心地舔著她的草莓味棒棒糖，臉上掛著黏黏的笑容。
名詞「棒棒糖」。

形容詞「鬆的、寬鬆的」。常用片語 break loose (掙脫)；let loose (放鬆、解放)。注意與動詞 lose (失去) 的拼寫區別。
After losing weight, his favorite pair of jeans became too loose, so he had to buy a smaller size.
減重後，他最喜歡的牛仔褲變得太寬鬆了，所以他不得不買小一號的。
形容詞「寬鬆的」。
The dog managed to break loose from its leash and ran excitedly across the park, chasing after squirrels.
這隻狗設法掙脫了皮帶，興奮地跑過公園，追逐松鼠。
片語 break loose 表「掙脫」。
The screws on the old chair were loose, making it wobble dangerously every time someone sat down.
舊椅子上的螺絲鬆了，每次有人坐下時都會危險地搖晃。
形容詞「鬆的」。
Related: loosen, loosely

名詞「君主、領主、上帝」。在歷史或宗教文本中常見。常用於稱呼上帝 (the Lord)。
In medieval times, a powerful lord ruled over a vast territory, protecting his subjects and collecting taxes.
在中世紀，一位強大的領主統治著廣闊的領土，保護他的臣民並徵收稅款。
名詞「領主、君主」。
Many people find comfort in prayer, believing that the Lord listens to their pleas and offers guidance during difficult times.
許多人在禱告中找到慰藉，相信上帝會傾聽他們的請求並在困難時期提供指引。
名詞「上帝」。通常大寫 "the Lord"。
Related: lordship

名詞「失敗者」。通常帶有負面或貶義。在口語或非正式語境中常見。
Despite being called a loser by some after his initial failures, the inventor eventually achieved great success through perseverance.
儘管最初的失敗讓他被一些人稱為失敗者，這位發明家最終透過毅力取得了巨大的成功。
名詞「失敗者」。
Related: lose, loss

名詞「愛人、愛好者」。常用片語 animal lover (愛護動物者)；music lover (音樂愛好者)。
The couple exchanged heartfelt vows, promising to be each other's faithful lovers and companions for the rest of their lives.
這對夫婦交換了衷心的誓言，承諾在餘生中成為彼此忠實的愛人和伴侶。
名詞「愛人」。
As a passionate animal lover, she volunteers at the local shelter every weekend, caring for abandoned cats and dogs.
作為一個熱情的動物愛好者，她每個週末都在當地收容所做志工，照顧被遺棄的貓狗。
名詞「愛好者」。常與其他名詞組成複合名詞，如 animal lover, book lover。
Related: love, loving

名詞「行李」。為不可數名詞，不可加 s。常用片語 a piece of luggage (一件行李)。
Before boarding the plane, passengers must check their luggage at the counter and ensure it meets the airline's weight restrictions.
登機前，乘客必須在櫃檯託運行李，並確保其符合航空公司的重量限制。
名詞「行李」，不可數。
He struggled to carry two heavy pieces of luggage up the stairs to his room after a long international flight.
經過漫長的國際航班後，他費力地將兩件沉重的行李搬上樓梯到他的房間。
luggage 是不可數名詞，若要表示數量，需用 a piece of luggage 或 two pieces of luggage。

名詞「肺」。常用於醫學或健康相關語境。
Smoking can severely damage a person's lungs over time, leading to serious respiratory diseases like emphysema.
長期吸菸會嚴重損害一個人的肺部，導致肺氣腫等嚴重的呼吸道疾病。
名詞「肺」。常用複數形式 lungs。

形容詞「神奇的，有魔力的」。常用來形容事物具有不可思議、令人驚嘆的特質。注意與 magic (名詞「魔術，魔法」或形容詞「魔術的」) 的區別。
The children were captivated by the magical performance, watching in awe as the illusionist made objects disappear and reappear on stage.
孩子們被這場神奇的表演迷住了，敬畏地看著魔術師在舞台上讓物體消失又重新出現。
形容詞「神奇的」。常用於描述表演、故事或體驗。
With a bit of creativity and teamwork, the students turned the ordinary classroom into a magical wonderland for the annual school festival.
憑藉一點創造力和團隊合作，學生們將普通的教室變成了年度校園節日的神奇仙境。
形容詞「神奇的」。常與 wonderland, moment, touch 等詞搭配。
Related: magic

名詞「魔術師」。指表演魔術或擁有魔法能力的人。注意與 wizard (巫師，通常指有真正魔法能力的人) 的區別。
The talented magician pulled a rabbit out of his hat, astonishing the audience with his skillful trick.
這位才華洋溢的魔術師從帽子裡變出了一隻兔子，他精湛的戲法讓觀眾驚訝不已。
名詞「魔術師」。常用動詞 pull out of (從...中取出)。
For the school's talent show, John practiced for weeks to perfect his card tricks, hoping to impress everyone like a professional magician.
為了學校的才藝表演，約翰練習了數週以完善他的撲克牌戲法，希望能像專業魔術師一樣讓大家印象深刻。
名詞「魔術師」。常用於描述表演魔術的人。
Related: magic, magical

名詞「磁鐵；有吸引力的人/物」。指能產生磁場並吸引鐵、鎳等金屬的物體，或比喻具有強大吸引力的人或地方。
The science teacher used a strong magnet to demonstrate how magnetic fields work by attracting small metal objects.
科學老師用一塊強磁鐵來演示磁場如何透過吸引小金屬物體來運作。
名詞「磁鐵」。常用於科學實驗或教學。
With its beautiful scenery and vibrant culture, the city has become a magnet for tourists from all over the world.
憑藉其美麗的風景和充滿活力的文化，這座城市已成為吸引世界各地遊客的磁石。
名詞「有吸引力的人/物」。常用片語 a magnet for sth/sb。
Related: magnetic, magnetism

名詞「女僕；未婚女子」。主要指在家庭中從事家務勞動的女性，或舊時指未婚女子 (如 maid of honor)。
The wealthy family hired a maid to help with the daily chores, such as cleaning the house and preparing meals.
這戶富裕人家僱用了一位女僕來協助處理日常家務，例如打掃房子和準備飯菜。
名詞「女僕」。常用於描述家庭服務人員。
At the wedding, the bride's best friend proudly served as the maid of honor, assisting her throughout the special day.
在婚禮上，新娘最好的朋友驕傲地擔任伴娘，在整個特別的日子裡協助她。
名詞片語 maid of honor「伴娘」。固定用法。

名詞「大多數，大部分」。指一個群體中數量較大的一部分。常用片語 a majority of (大多數的...)。注意與 minority (少數) 相對。
The majority of students voted for the class trip to the amusement park, showing a clear preference over the museum visit.
大多數學生投票支持班級旅行去遊樂園，顯示出對博物館參觀的明顯偏好。
名詞「大多數」。常用片語 a majority of + N (複數動詞或單數動詞依N而定)。
Despite some opposition, the proposal was approved by a slim majority in the student council, indicating close voting results.
儘管有些反對，這項提案仍以學生會的微弱多數通過，顯示投票結果非常接近。
名詞「大多數」。常用形容詞：large/vast majority (絕大多數), slim/bare majority (微弱多數)。
Related: major

名詞「購物中心」。指大型的室內購物場所，通常包含多家商店、餐廳和娛樂設施。
On weekends, many teenagers enjoy hanging out at the local shopping mall, browsing stores and catching movies with friends.
週末時，許多青少年喜歡在當地購物中心閒逛，逛商店並和朋友看電影。
名詞「購物中心」。常用搭配：shopping mall。
The new mall features a wide range of international brands and a large food court, attracting shoppers from neighboring towns.
這座新的購物中心擁有多種國際品牌和一個大型美食廣場，吸引了來自鄰近城鎮的購物者。
名詞「購物中心」。常用動詞 features (以...為特色)。

名詞「人類」。指全體人類，通常用於較為正式或哲學的語境。humankind 是更為性別中立的替代詞，兩者可互換。
Throughout history, mankind has always strived for progress, developing new technologies and expanding its understanding of the universe.
縱觀歷史，人類總是不斷追求進步，發展新科技並拓展對宇宙的理解。
名詞「人類」。通常視為單數，動詞用單數。
Protecting the environment is a crucial responsibility for all of humankind to ensure a sustainable future for generations to come.
保護環境是全人類的關鍵責任，以確保後代有一個永續的未來。
名詞「人類」。humankind 是 mankind 的性別中立替代詞。
Related: human

名詞「大理石；彈珠」。指一種常用於建築和雕塑的變質岩，或指兒童玩的玻璃彈珠。
The ancient Greek statues were carved from white marble, showcasing the artists' incredible skill and attention to detail.
古希臘雕像由白色大理石雕刻而成，展現了藝術家們令人難以置信的技巧和對細節的關注。
名詞「大理石」。常用於描述建築材料或藝術品。
During recess, the children gathered in the playground to play with their colorful marbles, rolling them across the ground.
下課時，孩子們聚集在操場上玩他們五顏六色的彈珠，讓它們在地上滾動。
名詞「彈珠」。常用複數形式 marbles。

名詞「行進，遊行」；動詞「行進，遊行」。指有組織地步行前進，通常是為了示威或慶祝。
Thousands of people joined the peaceful march for climate action, demanding that governments take immediate steps to protect the planet.
數千人參加了和平的氣候行動遊行，要求政府立即採取措施保護地球。
名詞「遊行」。常用片語 a march for sth。
The soldiers marched in perfect formation during the military parade, impressing the spectators with their discipline and coordination.
士兵們在閱兵典禮上以完美的隊形行進，他們紀律嚴明、協調一致的表現給觀眾留下了深刻印象。
動詞「行進」。常用於描述士兵或團體有組織地前進。
Students decided to march against the proposed tuition fee increase, hoping their collective voice would influence the university's decision.
學生們決定遊行反對擬議的學費調漲，希望他們的集體聲音能影響大學的決定。
動詞「遊行」。常用片語 march against sth (反對某事而遊行)。
Related: marching

名詞「麥克筆；標記；指標」。指一種粗頭筆，或用來標示位置、事件或重要性的物體或符號。
The teacher used a red marker to highlight the important points on the whiteboard, making it easier for students to take notes.
老師用紅色麥克筆標示出白板上的重點，讓學生更容易做筆記。
名詞「麥克筆」。常用於書寫或標記。
This ancient monument serves as a historical marker, reminding visitors of the significant events that occurred centuries ago in this region.
這座古老的紀念碑作為一個歷史標記，提醒遊客該地區幾個世紀前發生的重大事件。
名詞「標記，指標」。常用於表示地點、事件或重要性。
Related: mark

形容詞「極好的，了不起的」。常用來表達讚嘆或驚訝。注意其拼寫，尤其是 -ous 結尾。
The student's innovative science project, which explored sustainable energy solutions, received a marvelous score from the judges and won first prize.
這位學生探索永續能源解決方案的創新科學專案，獲得了評審極高的分數並贏得首獎。
形容詞「極好的，了不起的」。常用於描述令人印象深刻的事物。
Related: marvel, marvelously

形容詞「數學的」。常用於描述與數學相關的概念、方法或工具。注意其字尾 -ical。
Solving complex physics problems often requires a strong understanding of mathematical principles and logical reasoning skills.
解決複雜的物理問題通常需要對數學原理有深入的理解和邏輯推理能力。
形容詞「數學的」。常用搭配：mathematical equation (數學方程式), mathematical model (數學模型)。
Related: mathematics, mathematician, mathematically

名詞「市長」。指城市或城鎮的最高行政長官。注意其單複數形式。
The city mayor announced a new initiative to promote local tourism, hoping to attract more visitors and boost the economy.
市長宣布了一項推廣當地觀光的新計畫，希望能吸引更多遊客並提振經濟。
名詞「市長」。常用搭配：city mayor (市長), election for mayor (市長選舉)。
Related: mayoral

名詞「草地，牧場」。指一片長滿草的開闊地，常用於自然景觀描述。
During our hike in the national park, we stumbled upon a beautiful meadow filled with wildflowers and various species of butterflies.
在國家公園健行時，我們偶然發現了一片開滿野花和各種蝴蝶的美麗草地。
名詞「草地」。常用搭配：green meadow (綠草地), mountain meadow (山間草地)。

形容詞「有意義的，意味深長的」。常用於描述有重要性或價值的經驗、對話或關係。
The students engaged in a meaningful discussion about climate change, sharing diverse perspectives and proposing potential solutions.
學生們就氣候變遷進行了一場有意義的討論，分享了不同的觀點並提出了潛在的解決方案。
形容詞「有意義的」。常用搭配：meaningful experience (有意義的經驗), meaningful relationship (有意義的關係)。
Related: meaning, meaningfully

副詞「同時，在此期間」。常用來連接兩個同時發生或在同一時間段內發生的事件。也可作名詞，但較少見。
The scientists were busy conducting experiments in the lab; meanwhile, their colleagues were preparing the research findings for publication.
科學家們忙著在實驗室進行實驗；同時，他們的同事正在準備發表研究成果。
副詞「同時」。常用於連接兩個同時進行的動作或事件。
The first half of the concert was a bit slow, but the second half, the meanwhile, featured some truly spectacular performances.
演唱會上半場有點慢，但下半場，也就是在此期間，卻有一些真正精彩的表演。
名詞「同時，在此期間」。此用法較少見，但仍存在。

名詞「獎章，勳章」。常用於表彰成就或紀念事件。注意與「metal (金屬)」的拼寫區別。
After years of dedicated training, the athlete finally won a gold medal in the international swimming competition, fulfilling her lifelong dream.
經過多年的刻苦訓練，這位運動員終於在國際游泳比賽中贏得了一枚金牌，實現了她畢生的夢想。
名詞「獎章」。常用搭配：gold/silver/bronze medal (金/銀/銅牌), win a medal (贏得獎牌)。
Related: medalist

名詞「媒體；媒介；中等」。 「媒體」時，單數為 medium，複數為 media。但現代英語中，media 也常被當作集合名詞，直接指稱「媒體」。 「媒介」時，指傳播訊息或達到目的的工具。 「中等」時，指大小、程度居中。
Social media has become a powerful tool for communication, allowing people to share information and connect with others globally.
社群媒體已成為強大的溝通工具，讓人們能夠分享資訊並與全球各地的人們建立連結。
名詞「媒體」（複數形式，常作集合名詞）。常用搭配：social media (社群媒體), mass media (大眾媒體)。
The artist chose oil paint as her medium of expression, creating vibrant landscapes with rich textures and deep colors.
這位藝術家選擇油畫作為她的表達媒介，創作出色彩鮮豔、紋理豐富的風景畫。
名詞「媒介，方法」。指用於表達或傳播的工具或方式。
The student ordered a medium-sized coffee, which was just right for her study session without being too much or too little.
這位學生點了一杯中杯咖啡，份量剛好適合她的學習時段，不多也不少。
名詞「中等」。常用於描述尺寸、程度等。

名詞「瓜」。泛指各種瓜類水果，如西瓜、哈密瓜等。
During the hot summer months, a slice of chilled watermelon, a type of melon, is a refreshing and popular snack for many people.
在炎熱的夏季，一片冰鎮西瓜（一種瓜類）是許多人清爽又受歡迎的點心。
名詞「瓜」。常用搭配：watermelon (西瓜), cantaloupe (哈密瓜)。

動詞「融化，熔化」。指固體因受熱而變成液體。也可引申為「消失，軟化」。
The ice cream started to melt quickly under the scorching sun, so we had to eat it before it turned into a liquid puddle.
冰淇淋在烈日下迅速融化，所以我們必須在它變成一灘液體之前吃掉。
動詞「融化」。常用於描述冰、雪、巧克力等固體變液體。
His cold demeanor began to melt as he listened to the heartfelt apology from his friend, showing a hint of forgiveness.
當他聽到朋友真誠的道歉時，他冷淡的態度開始軟化，顯露出了一絲寬恕。
動詞「軟化，消失」（引申義）。常用於描述情緒或態度上的轉變。
Related: melted, melting

動詞「修補、修復」。常用於修補衣物、關係或損壞的東西。注意其過去式和過去分詞為 mended。片語 *mend one's ways* 指「改過自新」。
My grandmother carefully mended the torn shirt, making it look almost new again, which saved us from buying a replacement.
我的祖母仔細地修補了那件撕破的襯衫，讓它看起來幾乎像新的一樣，這省去了我們買一件新的。
動詞「修補（衣物）」。常用於修補紡織品。
After their big argument, the two friends tried to mend their broken relationship by talking openly and apologizing to each other.
在他們大吵一架之後，這兩個朋友試圖透過坦誠交談和互相道歉來修復他們破裂的關係。
動詞「修復（關係）」。常用搭配：mend a relationship/fence。
The student promised to mend his ways and study harder after receiving a warning about his poor academic performance.
這名學生在收到關於他學業表現不佳的警告後，承諾會改過自新並更努力學習。
片語 mend one's ways 指「改過自新」。
Related: mended, mending

形容詞「心理的、精神的」。常用於描述與心智、思想相關的事物，如 mental health (心理健康)、mental illness (精神疾病)。注意與 physical (身體的) 相對。
Maintaining good mental health is crucial for students to cope with academic pressure and daily challenges effectively.
維持良好的心理健康對學生有效應對學業壓力和日常挑戰至關重要。
形容詞「心理的、精神的」。常用搭配：mental health (心理健康)。
Solving complex puzzles and reading challenging books are excellent ways to provide mental exercise and keep the brain active.
解決複雜的謎題和閱讀有挑戰性的書籍是提供心智鍛鍊並保持大腦活躍的絕佳方式。
形容詞「心智的」。常用搭配：mental exercise (心智鍛鍊)。
The teacher observed that the student possessed exceptional mental abilities, allowing him to grasp difficult concepts quickly.
老師觀察到這名學生擁有卓越的心智能力，使他能快速理解困難的概念。
形容詞「心智的」。常用搭配：mental ability (心智能力)。
Related: mentally, mentality

形容詞「歡樂的、愉快的」。常用於節慶或歡樂的場合，如 Merry Christmas。片語 *make merry* 指「盡情歡樂」。
Everyone wished each other a Merry Christmas and exchanged gifts, spreading joy and warmth throughout the festive season.
大家互相祝賀聖誕快樂並交換禮物，在整個節慶期間散播歡樂和溫暖。
形容詞「歡樂的」。常用於節慶問候。
After the final exams, the students decided to make merry at a karaoke bar, celebrating the end of a stressful semester.
期末考結束後，學生們決定在卡拉OK酒吧盡情歡樂，慶祝一個緊張學期的結束。
片語 make merry 指「盡情歡樂」。
The children were in a merry mood as they played games and sang songs at the birthday party, enjoying every moment.
孩子們在生日派對上玩遊戲、唱歌，心情愉快，享受著每一個時刻。
形容詞「愉快的」。常用搭配：merry mood (愉快的心情)。
Related: merrily, merriment

名詞「混亂、髒亂」或動詞「弄亂」。名詞常用片語 *make a mess* (弄得一團糟)、*in a mess* (一團糟)。動詞常用 *mess up* (搞砸)。
The children made a huge mess in the living room with their toys and snacks, requiring an hour to clean up.
孩子們用他們的玩具和零食把客廳弄得一團糟，需要一個小時才能清理乾淨。
名詞「髒亂」。片語 make a mess 指「弄得一團糟」。
After the party, the entire kitchen was in a mess, with dirty dishes piled high and food crumbs everywhere.
派對過後，整個廚房一團糟，髒盤子堆得很高，食物碎屑到處都是。
名詞「混亂」。片語 in a mess 指「一團糟」。
He was worried he would mess up his presentation if he didn't practice enough, so he rehearsed it repeatedly.
他擔心如果練習不夠，他會搞砸他的簡報，所以他反覆排練。
動詞片語 mess up 指「搞砸」。
Related: messy

名詞「麥克風」。常用縮寫為 mike。在公開演講、唱歌或錄音時使用。
The speaker adjusted the microphone before starting his lecture, ensuring everyone in the large auditorium could hear him clearly.
演講者在開始演講前調整了麥克風，確保大禮堂裡的每個人都能清楚地聽到他的聲音。
名詞「麥克風」。常用動詞搭配：adjust, speak into。
During the concert, the lead singer accidentally dropped his mike, but a crew member quickly handed him another one.
演唱會期間，主唱不小心掉了他的麥克風，但一名工作人員迅速遞給他另一個。
名詞「麥克風」的縮寫。
For the outdoor event, they rented wireless microphones to allow the performers more freedom of movement on stage.
為了戶外活動，他們租用了無線麥克風，讓表演者在舞台上有更大的移動自由。
名詞「麥克風」。常用搭配：wireless microphone (無線麥克風)。

名詞「微波爐」或動詞「微波」。作為動詞時，指用微波爐加熱食物。
She quickly heated her leftover pizza in the microwave for a few minutes, making it warm and ready to eat.
她迅速將剩下的披薩在微波爐裡加熱了幾分鐘，讓它變熱並準備好食用。
名詞「微波爐」。常用動詞搭配：heat in the microwave。
Students often microwave instant noodles or frozen meals in their dorm rooms when they are short on time.
學生們時間不夠時，經常在宿舍裡微波泡麵或冷凍餐點。
動詞「微波」。指用微波爐加熱。
Before moving into her new apartment, she decided to buy a microwave to make cooking and reheating food more convenient.
在搬進新公寓之前，她決定買一台微波爐，讓烹飪和加熱食物更方便。
名詞「微波爐」。

形容詞「強大的、巨大的」。常用於描述力量、規模或重要性。口語中也可作副詞，表示「非常、很」。
The ancient empire was known for its mighty army and vast territories, dominating much of the known world for centuries.
這個古老帝國以其強大的軍隊和廣闊的領土而聞名，統治了已知世界的大部分地區數個世紀。
形容詞「強大的」。常用搭配：mighty army/power。
A mighty storm swept across the coast, causing widespread damage and power outages throughout the entire region.
一場巨大的風暴席捲海岸，在整個地區造成廣泛的損害和停電。
形容詞「巨大的、猛烈的」。
It took a mighty effort from the entire team to complete the challenging project before the tight deadline.
整個團隊付出了巨大的努力，才在緊迫的截止日期前完成了這個具有挑戰性的專案。
形容詞「巨大的、費力的」。常用搭配：mighty effort。
Related: mightily

介係詞「減去」、名詞「負號」或形容詞「負的」。作為介係詞時，常用於數學運算。作為形容詞時，指「負數的」或「缺少」。
In the math class, the teacher explained that ten minus three equals seven, demonstrating the basic subtraction operation.
在數學課上，老師解釋說十減三等於七，演示了基本的減法運算。
介係詞「減去」。常用於數學運算。
The temperature dropped to five degrees minus last night, indicating a very cold winter evening.
昨晚氣溫降到零下五度，表示一個非常寒冷的冬夜。
名詞「負號、零下」。常用搭配：minus five degrees (零下五度)。
Students who submit their assignments late will receive minus points, affecting their overall grade for the course.
遲交作業的學生將會被扣分，影響他們該課程的總成績。
形容詞「負的、扣分的」。常用搭配：minus points (扣分)。

名詞「奇蹟」。指無法解釋或令人驚訝的事件。常用片語 *work a miracle* (創造奇蹟)。
It was a miracle that no one was seriously injured in the car accident, despite the severe damage to both vehicles.
儘管兩輛車都嚴重受損，但車禍中沒有人受重傷，這真是一個奇蹟。
名詞「奇蹟」。常用搭配：a miracle that...。
The doctor said it would take a miracle for the patient to recover fully, given the severity of her condition.
醫生說，考慮到病情的嚴重性，病人要完全康復需要奇蹟。
片語 work a miracle 指「創造奇蹟」。
Scientists are still searching for a miracle cure for cancer, hoping to find a treatment that can save millions of lives.
科學家仍在尋找癌症的奇蹟療法，希望能找到一種能拯救數百萬生命的治療方法。
名詞「奇蹟」。常用搭配：miracle cure (奇蹟療法)。
Related: miraculous, miraculously

名詞「悲慘、痛苦」。指極度的不快樂或不幸。常用片語 *put someone out of their misery* (結束某人的痛苦)。
Many people in war-torn regions live in extreme misery, facing hunger, displacement, and constant danger every day.
許多飽受戰爭蹂躪地區的人們生活在極度悲慘之中，每天面臨飢餓、流離失所和持續的危險。
名詞「悲慘、痛苦」。常用搭配：live in misery (生活在痛苦中)。
The prolonged drought caused widespread misery among farmers, as their crops withered and their livelihoods were threatened.
長期的乾旱給農民帶來了廣泛的痛苦，因為他們的莊稼枯萎，生計受到威脅。
名詞「痛苦」。常用動詞搭配：cause misery (造成痛苦)。
The student found the difficult math problem to be such a misery that he wished the teacher would just give him the answer.
這名學生覺得這道數學難題如此痛苦，他希望老師能直接告訴他答案。
名詞「痛苦」。口語中也可指令人不快或煩惱的事物。
Related: miserable, miserably

飛彈、導彈。常用於軍事或科技新聞。注意其複數形為 missiles。
The country successfully tested a new long-range missile, demonstrating its advanced defense capabilities to the world.
該國成功試射了一枚新型長程飛彈，向世界展示其先進的國防能力。
名詞「飛彈」。常用搭配：long-range missile (長程飛彈), ballistic missile (彈道飛彈), cruise missile (巡弋飛彈)。
Related: missiles

形容詞「失蹤的、遺失的」。常用於人或物品的消失。注意其動詞是 miss (想念/錯過)。
After the earthquake, several people were reported missing, and rescue teams immediately launched a large-scale search operation.
地震後，有數人被通報失蹤，搜救隊立即展開大規模搜索行動。
形容詞「失蹤的」。常用搭配：be reported missing (被通報失蹤), go missing (失蹤)。
The detective found a crucial piece of evidence missing from the crime scene, which complicated the entire investigation.
偵探發現犯罪現場缺少了一項關鍵證據，這使整個調查變得複雜。
形容詞「遺失的、缺少的」。常用搭配：a missing piece (遺失的一塊), something is missing (某物不見了)。

名詞「任務、使命」。可指軍事任務、太空任務，或個人的重要使命。
The astronaut's primary mission was to collect rock samples from the moon's surface and conduct various scientific experiments.
這位太空人的主要任務是從月球表面採集岩石樣本並進行各種科學實驗。
名詞「任務」。常用搭配：a space mission (太空任務), a diplomatic mission (外交任務)。
As a teacher, she believes her lifelong mission is to inspire young minds and foster a love for learning in her students.
作為一名老師，她相信她一生的使命是啟發年輕的心靈並培養學生對學習的熱愛。
名詞「使命、天職」。常用搭配：a sense of mission (使命感), on a mission (身負任務)。
Related: missions

名詞「薄霧、水氣」；動詞「使蒙上薄霧、使模糊」。與 fog (濃霧) 相比，mist 通常較薄。
A thick mist rolled in from the ocean, making it difficult for drivers to see the road clearly during their morning commute.
一層濃厚的薄霧從海洋飄來，使得駕駛在早上通勤時難以清楚看見道路。
名詞「薄霧」。常用搭配：a thick mist (濃霧), mist rolls in (薄霧飄來)。
The cold air caused the car windows to mist up, so the driver had to turn on the defroster to improve visibility.
寒冷的空氣導致車窗蒙上薄霧，所以司機必須打開除霧器以改善能見度。
動詞片語「蒙上薄霧」。常用搭配：mist up (蒙上薄霧)。
Related: misted, misting, mists, misty

名詞「暴民、烏合之眾」；動詞「圍攻、蜂擁而上」。通常帶有負面或混亂的意味。
The angry mob stormed the city hall, protesting against the new policy and demanding immediate changes from the government.
憤怒的暴民衝進市政廳，抗議新政策並要求政府立即做出改變。
名詞「暴民」。常用搭配：an angry mob (憤怒的暴民), the mob mentality (暴民心態)。
After the concert, fans mobbed the rock star's car, hoping to get an autograph or a quick photo with their idol.
演唱會結束後，粉絲們蜂擁而上圍住這位搖滾明星的車，希望能得到簽名或與偶像快速合照。
動詞「圍攻、蜂擁而上」。常用搭配：be mobbed by (被...圍攻)。
Related: mobbed, mobbing, mobs

形容詞「可移動的、行動的」。常用於描述物品或服務的移動性。注意名詞形式 mobility (移動性)。
Modern smartphones are essentially mobile computers, allowing users to access information and communicate from anywhere.
現代智慧型手機本質上是行動電腦，讓使用者可以隨時隨地存取資訊和通訊。
形容詞「行動的、可移動的」。常用搭配：mobile phone (行動電話), mobile device (行動裝置), mobile library (行動圖書館)。
The company offers a mobile repair service, sending technicians directly to customers' homes to fix their appliances.
這家公司提供行動維修服務，派遣技術人員直接到客戶家中修理他們的電器。
形容詞「可移動的、流動的」。
Related: mobility

形容詞「潮濕的、濕潤的」。通常指輕微的濕潤，比 wet (濕透的) 程度輕。
The soil in the garden was still moist after the morning rain, which was perfect for planting new seedlings.
經過早晨的雨水，花園裡的土壤仍然潮濕，這非常適合種植新幼苗。
形容詞「潮濕的、濕潤的」。常用搭配：moist soil (潮濕的土壤), moist air (潮濕的空氣)。
She applied a moist towel to her forehead to cool down after her intense workout at the gym.
她在健身房劇烈運動後，將一條濕毛巾敷在額頭上以降溫。
形容詞「濕潤的」。
Related: moisten, moisture

名詞「濕氣、水分」。通常指空氣中或物體內微量的水。不可數名詞。
The desert air lacks moisture, which makes it challenging for many plants and animals to survive in such a harsh environment.
沙漠空氣缺乏水分，這使得許多動植物難以在如此惡劣的環境中生存。
名詞「濕氣、水分」。不可數名詞。常用搭配：lack moisture (缺乏水分), retain moisture (保持水分)。
To prevent the bread from drying out, it's important to seal it in an airtight container to lock in its moisture.
為了防止麵包變乾，重要的是將其密封在一個密閉容器中以鎖住其水分。
名詞「水分」。
Related: moist

名詞「僧侶、修道士」。指在寺廟或修道院過著宗教生活的人。
The Buddhist monk dedicated his life to meditation and studying ancient scriptures, seeking spiritual enlightenment.
這位佛教僧侶將一生奉獻給冥想和研讀古老經文，以尋求精神上的啟蒙。
名詞「僧侶」。常用搭配：Buddhist monk (佛教僧侶), Christian monk (基督教修道士)。
Visitors to the remote temple often observe the monks performing their daily rituals, which include chanting and silent prayer.
參觀偏遠寺廟的遊客經常觀察僧侶們進行日常儀式，其中包括誦經和靜默祈禱。
名詞「僧侶」的複數形。
Related: monks

名詞「怪物、巨獸」。可指神話中的生物，或形容巨大、可怕的事物。
According to ancient legends, a terrifying monster dwelled in the deep lake, occasionally emerging to frighten local villagers.
根據古老傳說，一隻可怕的怪物棲息在深湖中，偶爾會現身嚇唬當地村民。
名詞「怪物」。常用搭配：a terrifying monster (可怕的怪物), sea monster (海怪)。
The new skyscraper is a concrete monster, dominating the city skyline and blocking out the sunlight for nearby residents.
這座新摩天大樓是一個混凝土巨獸，主宰著城市天際線並遮蔽了附近居民的陽光。
名詞「巨大而可怕的事物」。此處為比喻用法。
Related: monstrous

monthly 可作形容詞「每月的」、副詞「每月地」和名詞「月刊/月費」。作為名詞時，通常指定期出版的刊物或固定支付的費用。
The student received a monthly allowance from her parents to cover her living expenses while studying abroad.
這位學生每月從父母那裡獲得零用錢，以支付她在國外學習期間的生活開銷。
形容詞「每月的」。常用搭配：monthly allowance (月零用錢), monthly payment (月付款)。
The company pays its employees monthly, ensuring they receive their salaries on the last working day of each month.
這家公司每月支付員工薪水，確保他們在每個月的最後一個工作日收到薪資。
副詞「每月地」。常與動詞搭配，表示動作發生的頻率。
Many academic journals are published as monthlies, providing researchers with regular updates on new findings in their fields.
許多學術期刊以月刊形式出版，為研究人員提供其領域內新發現的定期更新。
名詞「月刊」。指每月出版的刊物。類似用法還有 weekly (週刊), quarterly (季刊)。

moral 可作形容詞「道德的」和名詞「道德；寓意」。作為名詞時，可指個人或社會的道德標準，或故事、事件所蘊含的教訓。
The teacher emphasized the importance of making moral choices in daily life, even when faced with difficult situations.
老師強調在日常生活中做出道德選擇的重要性，即使面對困難的情況也是如此。
形容詞「道德的」。常用搭配：moral choice (道德選擇), moral principles (道德原則)。
The philosopher debated the morals of artificial intelligence, questioning its potential impact on human society and values.
這位哲學家辯論了人工智慧的道德問題，質疑其對人類社會和價值觀的潛在影響。
名詞「道德；倫理」。常用複數形式 morals 指道德規範或倫理觀念。片語 morals of sth。
The fable of the tortoise and the hare teaches a valuable moral about perseverance and not underestimating others' abilities.
龜兔賽跑的寓言教導了一個關於毅力且不要低估他人能力的重要寓意。
名詞「寓意；教訓」。指故事或事件所傳達的道理。
Related: morality, morally

mosquito 作為名詞，指「蚊子」。蚊子是常見的昆蟲，有些種類會傳播疾病，因此在公共衛生議題中常被提及。
To prevent the spread of dengue fever, residents were advised to eliminate breeding sites for mosquitoes around their homes.
為了預防登革熱傳播，居民被建議清除住家周圍的蚊子孳生源。
名詞「蚊子」。常用複數形式 mosquitoes。注意其與疾病傳播的關聯。
During the summer, we always use mosquito repellent when hiking in the mountains to avoid itchy bites and potential infections.
夏天時，我們在山區健行時總是使用防蚊液，以避免發癢的叮咬和潛在的感染。
名詞「蚊子」。常用搭配：mosquito repellent (防蚊液), mosquito net (蚊帳)。

mostly 作為副詞，意為「主要地；大部分地」。它表示某事物在很大程度上是真實的或某群體中的大部分成員具有某種特徵，但非全部。
The students in the advanced English class are mostly from different Asian countries, bringing diverse cultural perspectives to discussions.
這間進階英文班的學生主要來自不同的亞洲國家，為討論帶來多元的文化視角。
副詞「主要地；大部分地」。表示大部分成員的來源或組成。常用於修飾動詞或形容詞。
Although the project had some minor issues, the presentation was mostly successful, receiving positive feedback from the panel of judges.
儘管這個專案有一些小問題，但這次發表會大部分是成功的，獲得了評審團的正面回饋。
副詞「主要地；大部分地」。表示某事物的整體情況或結果。與 largely, mainly 意思相近。

motel 作為名詞，指「汽車旅館」。它是一種專為開車旅行者設計的住宿設施，通常提供直接從停車場進入房間的便利。
After a long day of driving, the family decided to stop at a motel by the highway for a much-needed rest before continuing their journey.
經過漫長一天的駕駛後，這家人決定在高速公路旁的一家汽車旅館停留，好好休息一下再繼續旅程。
名詞「汽車旅館」。通常位於公路旁，方便開車旅客入住。
Unlike traditional hotels, motels typically offer direct access to rooms from the parking lot, catering to travelers with cars.
與傳統飯店不同，汽車旅館通常提供從停車場直接進入房間的通道，以迎合開車旅行者的需求。
名詞「汽車旅館」。強調其與飯店的區別，特別是停車和房間出入的便利性。

moth 作為名詞，指「蛾」。蛾與蝴蝶相似，但通常在夜間活動，且翅膀在休息時平放或摺疊。
A large moth flew into the brightly lit room through the open window, attracted by the light on the ceiling.
一隻大蛾從開著的窗戶飛進燈火通明的房間，被天花板上的燈光吸引。
名詞「蛾」。指一種夜間活動的昆蟲，常被燈光吸引。
Some types of moths are known to damage clothes made of wool, so people use mothballs to protect their garments.
有些種類的蛾會損壞羊毛製的衣物，所以人們會使用樟腦丸來保護他們的服裝。
名詞「蛾」。提及某些蛾類對衣物的危害性。常用搭配：mothball (樟腦丸)。

motor 作為名詞，指「馬達；發動機」。它是將電能或其他形式的能量轉換為機械能的裝置，廣泛應用於各種機械和交通工具。
The mechanic quickly identified the problem as a faulty motor in the washing machine, which needed to be replaced immediately.
技師很快就查出問題是洗衣機的馬達故障，需要立即更換。
名詞「馬達；發動機」。指驅動機械運轉的裝置。常用搭配：faulty motor (故障馬達)。
Electric cars are powered by an electric motor, offering a more environmentally friendly alternative to traditional gasoline engines.
電動汽車由電動馬達驅動，提供比傳統汽油引擎更環保的替代方案。
名詞「馬達；發動機」。指提供動力的核心部件。常用搭配：electric motor (電動馬達)。
Related: motorized, motoring

multiply 作為動詞，意為「乘；增加；繁殖」。它有多重含義，可指數學上的乘法運算，數量上的顯著增長，或生物的繁衍。
In mathematics, students learn to multiply numbers to solve complex equations and understand quantitative relationships between values.
在數學中，學生學習如何將數字相乘，以解決複雜的方程式並理解數值之間的量化關係。
動詞「乘」。指數學上的乘法運算。常用搭配：multiply by (乘以)。
The company's profits began to multiply rapidly after they launched their innovative new product line last year, exceeding all expectations.
這家公司在去年推出創新產品線後，利潤開始迅速增長，超出了所有預期。
動詞「增加；增長」。指數量或程度上的顯著增加。常用於描述經濟或事業的發展。
Bacteria can multiply very quickly in warm, moist environments, leading to rapid growth of colonies and potential health issues.
細菌在溫暖潮濕的環境中能非常快速地繁殖，導致菌落迅速生長並可能引發健康問題。
動詞「繁殖」。指生物的繁衍或數量上的快速增加。常用於生物學或醫學語境。
Related: multiplication, multiple, multiplier

murder 可作名詞「謀殺」和動詞「謀殺」。它指非法且蓄意地奪走他人生命，是嚴重的刑事犯罪。
The police are investigating the brutal murder of a prominent businessman, searching for any clues or witnesses to the crime.
警方正在調查一名知名商人的殘酷謀殺案，尋找任何犯罪線索或目擊者。
名詞「謀殺」。指非法且蓄意致人於死的行為。常用搭配：murder of (某人的謀殺案)。
The detective suspected that the suspect had murdered his business partner to gain complete control of the company's assets.
偵探懷疑嫌犯謀殺了他的商業夥伴，以完全掌控公司的資產。
動詞「謀殺」。指蓄意殺害某人。注意動詞的時態變化。
Related: murderer, murderous

muscle 作為名詞，指「肌肉」。它是構成動物身體組織的一部分，負責產生運動、維持姿勢和產生熱量。常用於描述身體力量或健康。
Regular exercise helps to build strong muscles and improve overall physical fitness and endurance for daily activities.
規律運動有助於鍛鍊強壯的肌肉，並提升日常活動的整體體能和耐力。
名詞「肌肉」。指身體的肌肉組織。常用搭配：build muscles (鍛鍊肌肉), strong muscles (強壯的肌肉)。
After a strenuous workout, he felt a soreness in his leg muscles, indicating a good effort at the gym that day.
劇烈運動後，他感到腿部肌肉痠痛，這表示那天在健身房的努力很有成效。
名詞「肌肉」。指身體的特定部位肌肉。常用搭配：sore muscles (肌肉痠痛)。
Related: muscular

用法包括：(1) 名詞「蘑菇」。在GSAT中，除了指食物，也常考其動詞用法；(2) 動詞「迅速增加/擴展」。常用於形容數量、規模或影響力快速增長。
The chef carefully sliced the fresh mushrooms and added them to the pasta dish, enhancing its earthy flavor.
廚師小心地切片新鮮蘑菇，並將它們加入義大利麵中，增添了其泥土般的風味。
名詞「蘑菇」。指一種可食用的真菌。
After the new policy was implemented, small businesses in the area began to mushroom, creating many job opportunities for residents.
新政策實施後，該地區的小企業開始如雨後春筍般迅速增加，為居民創造了許多就業機會。
動詞「迅速增加/擴展」。常用於形容數量、規模或影響力快速增長，類似「如雨後春筍般」。
Related: mushrooms, mushrooming

名詞「謎團；神秘的事物」。常用搭配：solve a mystery, a sense of mystery, the mystery of life。注意其形容詞 mysterious。
The ancient ruins held a deep mystery for archaeologists, who spent years trying to decipher their purpose and origin.
這些古老遺跡對考古學家來說是一個深奧的謎團，他們花費數年試圖解讀其用途和起源。
名詞「謎團；難以理解的事物」。常用介係詞 for。
Despite extensive investigations, the disappearance of the famous explorer remains a complete mystery to this day.
儘管進行了廣泛的調查，這位著名探險家的失蹤至今仍是一個完全的謎團。
名詞「謎團」。常用搭配 complete mystery 或 solve a mystery。
Related: mysteries, mysterious, mysteriously

形容詞「裸體的；赤裸的」。常用於描述身體未被衣物覆蓋，或抽象地指「赤裸裸的真相/事實」。
The artist sketched the naked figure, focusing on the human form's natural curves and musculature to capture its essence.
藝術家勾勒出裸體人像，專注於人體形態的自然曲線和肌肉組織，以捕捉其精髓。
形容詞「裸體的」。指沒有穿衣服的狀態。
After the scandal broke, the company's true financial state was laid naked for everyone to see, revealing years of mismanagement.
醜聞爆發後，公司真實的財務狀況赤裸裸地呈現在眾人面前，揭示了多年的管理不善。
形容詞「赤裸裸的；無遮掩的」。常用於抽象地指真相或事實被揭露，無所隱藏。片語 lay naked for...。
Related: nakedly, nakedness

用法包括：(1) 名詞「小睡；打盹」。常用片語 take a nap；(2) 動詞「小睡；打盹」。
After a long morning of studying, the student decided to take a short nap to refresh her mind before the afternoon classes.
經過一個上午的長時間學習後，這位學生決定小睡片刻，以便在下午的課程前恢復精神。
名詞「小睡」。常用片語 take a nap。
The toddler often naps in the early afternoon, allowing his parents some quiet time to complete household chores.
這個學步兒童經常在下午早些時候小睡，讓他的父母有一些安靜的時間來完成家務。
動詞「小睡；打盹」。指短暫的睡眠。
Related: naps, napping, napped

名詞「餐巾」。在餐廳或用餐情境中常見的單字。
During the formal dinner, each guest carefully placed their cloth napkin on their lap before the first course was served.
在正式晚宴上，每位客人在上第一道菜之前，都小心翼翼地將布餐巾放在膝蓋上。
名詞「餐巾」。指用餐時擦嘴或保護衣物的布或紙。
The waiter quickly brought a stack of paper napkins to the table after a child accidentally spilled juice all over it.
一個孩子不小心把果汁灑了一桌後，服務生迅速拿來一疊紙餐巾。
名詞「餐巾」。常用搭配 paper napkin 或 cloth napkin。
Related: napkins

用法包括：(1) 形容詞「本地的；原生的；天生的」。常用於 native speaker, native land, native culture；(2) 名詞「本地人；原住民」。
Although she grew up abroad, she is a native speaker of Mandarin Chinese, having learned it from her parents.
儘管她在國外長大，但她是華語的母語使用者，因為她從小從父母那裡學會了華語。
形容詞「本地的；母語的」。常用片語 native speaker (母語使用者)。
The botanist discovered a rare native plant species that only grows in this specific high-altitude region of the mountains.
這位植物學家發現了一種稀有的原生植物物種，它只生長在山區這個特定的高海拔地區。
形容詞「原生的；本地的」。指某地特有的動植物。
As a native of Taipei, he can provide excellent recommendations for local eateries and hidden cultural gems.
作為一個台北本地人，他可以為當地餐館和隱藏的文化瑰寶提供很棒的建議。
名詞「本地人」。指在某地出生或居住的人。
Related: natives, natively

名詞「海軍」。常用於描述軍事組織或其顏色「海軍藍」。
The country's navy conducted a large-scale maritime exercise to demonstrate its readiness and protect its territorial waters.
該國海軍進行了一次大規模海上演習，以展示其戰備狀態並保護其領海。
名詞「海軍」。指一個國家的海上軍事力量。
For the school uniform, students are required to wear a white shirt with a navy blue blazer and grey trousers.
校服要求學生穿白色襯衫、海軍藍西裝外套和灰色長褲。
名詞「海軍藍」。指一種深藍色，常用於描述顏色。常用搭配 navy blue。
Related: navies, naval

形容詞「整潔的；俐落的；很棒的」。常用於形容環境整潔、動作俐落或事物很棒。
She always keeps her desk incredibly neat and organized, making it easy to find any document she needs instantly.
她總是把她的書桌整理得非常整潔有序，讓她能立刻找到所需的任何文件。
形容詞「整潔的；井然有序的」。
The gymnast performed a series of neat flips and twists, landing perfectly each time to the audience's applause.
這位體操選手完成了一系列俐落的翻轉和扭動，每次都完美落地，贏得了觀眾的掌聲。
形容詞「俐落的；熟練的」。指動作或執行方式乾淨、有效率。
Our teacher gave us a really neat idea for our science project, which involved building a miniature solar-powered car.
我們的老師給了我們一個非常棒的科學專案點子，內容是建造一輛微型太陽能汽車。
形容詞「很棒的；巧妙的」。口語中常用來表示某事很酷或很聰明。
Related: neatly, neatness

名詞「必需品；必要性」。常用片語 of necessity (必然地), a matter of necessity (必要之事)。
In remote areas, a reliable internet connection has become a basic necessity for education and communication.
在偏遠地區，可靠的網路連線已成為教育和通訊的基本必需品。
名詞「必需品」。指不可或缺的東西。常用搭配 basic necessity。
Due to the sudden change in weather, wearing a warm coat was a necessity rather than a choice for everyone outdoors.
由於天氣突然變化，對於戶外所有人來說，穿一件暖和的外套是必需而非選擇。
名詞「必要性」。指某事是必須的，而非可選擇的。常用片語 necessity rather than choice。
Related: necessities, necessary, necessarily

名詞「領帶」。常用於正式場合的服飾配件。
For the job interview, he made sure to wear a crisp shirt and a neatly tied necktie to make a good impression.
為了這次工作面試，他確保自己穿著一件挺括的襯衫和一條打理整齊的領帶，以留下好印象。
名詞「領帶」。指男士穿著襯衫時繫在頸部的裝飾品。
The school requires all male students to wear a uniform that includes a blue blazer and a striped necktie on formal occasions.
學校要求所有男學生在正式場合穿著包含藍色西裝外套和條紋領帶的制服。
名詞「領帶」。常用於描述正式服裝的一部分。
Related: neckties

名詞「鄰近地區、社區」。常用片語 in the neighborhood of (大約、在...附近)。注意與 community 的區別，neighborhood 強調地理上的鄰近，community 則強調共同的興趣或歸屬感。
The new park has significantly improved the quality of life for residents in the neighborhood, offering a safe space for children to play.
新公園顯著提升了社區居民的生活品質，為孩子們提供了安全的遊玩空間。
名詞「鄰近地區、社區」。強調地理上的範圍。
The old bookstore, a beloved landmark, has been a part of this quiet neighborhood for over fifty years, attracting readers from all walks of life.
這間古老的書店，一個受人喜愛的地標，已經是這個寧靜社區的一部分超過五十年了，吸引著各行各業的讀者。
名詞「社區」。常用搭配：quiet neighborhood, friendly neighborhood。
The total cost of the renovation project is expected to be in the neighborhood of NT$5 million, slightly higher than the initial estimate.
翻修專案的總成本預計在大約新台幣五百萬元左右，比最初的估計略高。
片語 in the neighborhood of sth 意為「大約、在...附近」。常用於估計數字。

名詞「鳥巢、窩」。動詞「築巢、棲息」。注意動詞用法常與介係詞搭配，如 nest in。
The tiny bird built its nest in the branches of the tall oak tree, carefully weaving twigs and leaves together.
這隻小鳥在高大的橡樹枝上築巢，仔細地將樹枝和樹葉編織在一起。
名詞「鳥巢」。常用搭配：build a nest, bird's nest。
Many migratory birds nest in this protected wetland area during the spring, finding abundant food and a safe environment.
許多候鳥在春天時會在這個受保護的濕地築巢，尋找豐富的食物和安全的環境。
動詞「築巢、棲息」。常用介係詞 in。
After years of hard work, they finally saved enough money to buy their own little nest, a cozy apartment in the city center.
經過多年的努力，他們終於存夠錢買了自己的小窩，一間位於市中心的舒適公寓。
名詞「窩」，引申為舒適的家。
Related: nesting

名詞「綽號、暱稱」。動詞「給...取綽號」。動詞用法常為 nickname sb (as) sth。
Due to his exceptional height, John's friends gave him the nickname of 'Giraffe' during their high school basketball days.
因為他非凡的身高，約翰的朋友們在他高中籃球時期給了他「長頸鹿」的綽號。
名詞「綽號」。常用搭配：give sb a nickname, the nickname of。
The talented young artist was nicknamed 'The Brush Master' by critics because of his unique painting style and incredible skill.
這位才華洋溢的年輕藝術家因為他獨特的繪畫風格和令人難以置信的技巧，被評論家們暱稱為「畫筆大師」。
動詞「給...取綽號」。常用被動語態 be nicknamed (as) sth。
Even though her real name was Elizabeth, everyone at school knew her by her cheerful nickname, 'Lizzy', which suited her personality perfectly.
即使她的真名是伊莉莎白，學校裡每個人都以她開朗的暱稱「莉茲」認識她，這個暱稱非常符合她的個性。
名詞「暱稱」。
Related: nicknamed, nicknaming

形容詞「正常的、一般的」。常用片語 back to normal (恢復正常)。注意副詞 normally。
After the power outage, it took a few hours for the electricity supply to return to normal in the entire residential area.
停電後，整個住宅區的電力供應花了幾個小時才恢復正常。
形容詞「正常的」。片語 back to normal 意為「恢復正常」。
It's completely normal for students to feel nervous before a big exam; what matters is how they manage that stress.
學生在大考前感到緊張是完全正常的；重要的是他們如何管理那種壓力。
形容詞「正常的」。常用句型 It's normal for sb to do sth。
Despite the initial challenges, the new project is now progressing at a normal pace, and we expect to meet all deadlines.
儘管最初面臨挑戰，新專案現在正以正常的速度推進，我們預計將會趕上所有截止日期。
形容詞「正常的、一般的」。常用搭配：normal pace, normal temperature。
Related: normally, normality

名詞「小說家」。由 novel (小說) 加上 -ist (表示從事某種職業的人) 構成。
The renowned novelist spent years researching historical events to ensure accuracy in her latest best-selling historical fiction.
這位著名的小說家花費數年研究歷史事件，以確保她最新暢銷歷史小說的準確性。
名詞「小說家」。常用搭配：renowned novelist, best-selling novelist。
As an aspiring novelist, he dedicated his evenings to writing, hoping to one day publish a book that would inspire readers.
作為一名有抱負的小說家，他將晚上時間投入寫作，希望有朝一日能出版一本能啟發讀者的書。
名詞「小說家」。

名詞「修女、尼姑」。指在修道院過宗教生活的女性。
The kind nun dedicated her life to helping the poor and sick, working tirelessly in the local orphanage and hospital.
這位善良的修女將一生奉獻給幫助窮人和病人，在當地的孤兒院和醫院不知疲倦地工作。
名詞「修女」。
She decided to become a nun after feeling a strong calling to serve God and live a life of spiritual devotion and simplicity.
她在感受到強烈的召喚去侍奉上帝並過著靈性奉獻和簡樸的生活後，決定成為一名修女。
名詞「修女」。

名詞「橡樹；橡木」。常用於指稱樹木或其木材。
The ancient oak tree in the schoolyard has stood there for centuries, providing shade for generations of students.
校園裡那棵古老的橡樹已經矗立了數百年，為好幾代的學生提供了樹蔭。
名詞「橡樹」。
The antique dining table, made from solid oak, was passed down through generations, a cherished family heirloom.
這張由實心橡木製成的古董餐桌代代相傳，是珍貴的傳家寶。
名詞「橡木」。

動詞「觀察、遵守、慶祝」。注意其多重意義及搭配介係詞。
Scientists observed the behavior of the rare bird in its natural habitat, documenting its feeding and nesting patterns.
科學家們觀察了這種稀有鳥類在其自然棲息地中的行為，記錄了牠們的覓食和築巢模式。
動詞「觀察」。常用搭配：observe behavior, observe phenomenon。
All citizens are expected to observe the traffic laws strictly to ensure safety on the roads.
所有公民都應嚴格遵守交通法規，以確保道路安全。
動詞「遵守」。常用搭配：observe rules/laws/customs。
The community will observe the annual harvest festival next month with various cultural performances and traditional foods.
社區將於下個月舉辦年度豐收節，屆時將有各種文化表演和傳統食物。
動詞「慶祝、紀念」。常用搭配：observe a holiday/festival/anniversary。
Related: observation, observer, observable

名詞「場合、時機」。常用片語 on occasion (偶爾)、on the occasion of (在...之際)。
The graduation ceremony was a memorable occasion for all the students and their families, marking the end of an important chapter.
畢業典禮對所有學生及其家人來說都是一個難忘的場合，標誌著一個重要篇章的結束。
名詞「場合、盛事」。常用搭配：special occasion, memorable occasion。
He only wears a suit and tie on special occasions, preferring casual clothes for everyday activities.
他只在特殊場合穿西裝打領帶，日常活動更喜歡休閒服裝。
名詞「場合」。片語 on occasion(s) 意為「偶爾、有時」。
The principal gave a inspiring speech on the occasion of the school's 60th anniversary, reflecting on its history and future.
校長在學校六十週年校慶之際發表了鼓舞人心的演講，回顧了學校的歷史和展望未來。
片語 on the occasion of sth 意為「在...之際」。
Related: occasional, occasionally

形容詞「奇怪的、奇數的、零星的」。注意其多重意義。
It was an odd coincidence that both friends wore the exact same shirt to the party without planning it.
兩個朋友在沒有事先約定的情況下，都穿了完全相同的襯衫去派對，這是一個奇怪的巧合。
形容詞「奇怪的、不尋常的」。
In this math game, players must only select the odd numbers from the list to score points.
在這個數學遊戲中，玩家必須只從列表中選擇奇數才能得分。
形容詞「奇數的」。與 even (偶數的) 相對。
After graduating, he took several odd jobs to save money for his dream trip around the world.
畢業後，他做了幾份零工，為他的環遊世界夢想之旅存錢。
片語 odd jobs 意為「零工、雜務」。
Related: oddly, oddness

動詞「省略、遺漏」。常用於正式語境。注意其名詞形 omission。常考被動語態 be omitted。
Due to the strict word limit, the editor decided to omit some less crucial details from the article to make it more concise.
由於嚴格的字數限制，編輯決定從文章中省略一些不那麼重要的細節，使其更為簡潔。
動詞「省略」。常搭配 from。
Students who omit important steps in their math problems often get incorrect answers, even if their final calculation is close.
在數學問題中省略重要步驟的學生，即使最終計算接近，也常會得到錯誤的答案。
動詞「遺漏」。
Related: omission, omitted, omitting

形容詞「持續進行中的」。通常指某個活動、過程或事件仍在發生，尚未結束。常與 process, project, discussion 等名詞搭配。
The school board is holding an ongoing discussion about the new curriculum, gathering feedback from teachers and parents.
校務委員會正在就新課程進行持續的討論，收集老師和家長的意見。
形容詞「持續進行中的」。常用於描述一個尚未結束的過程或活動。
Despite the challenges, the construction of the new library remains an ongoing project, expected to be completed next year.
儘管面臨挑戰，新圖書館的建設仍是一個持續進行中的項目，預計明年完工。
形容詞「持續的」。常修飾 project, process 等。

名詞「洋蔥」。指一種常見的蔬菜，有刺激性氣味，常用於烹飪。注意其複數形 onions。
To make the soup more flavorful, the chef finely chopped an onion and sautéed it until it was translucent and sweet.
為了讓湯更美味，廚師將一個洋蔥切碎，然後炒至半透明並帶有甜味。
名詞「洋蔥」。
Many people cry when they cut onions because of the irritating compounds released into the air.
許多人在切洋蔥時會哭泣，因為洋蔥會釋放出刺激性化合物到空氣中。
名詞「洋蔥」，複數形。
Related: onions

介系詞「到...上面」。表示移動到某物表面上，強調動作的方向和結果。與 on 相似，但 onto 更強調動態。
The cat gracefully jumped onto the bookshelf, carefully avoiding the stack of fragile books.
那隻貓優雅地跳到書架上，小心翼翼地避開了一疊易碎的書。
介系詞「到...上面」。表示從一個地方移動到另一個地方的表面。
Please place the documents onto the desk for the manager to review them before the meeting starts.
請將文件放到桌上，讓經理在會議開始前審閱。
介系詞「到...上面」。強調將物品放置到某個表面。

名詞「操作；運作；手術；軍事行動」。有多種意思，需根據上下文判斷。常考搭配：in operation (運作中), undergo an operation (動手術)。
The new software system will be in full operation by next month, significantly improving our data processing efficiency.
新軟體系統將於下個月全面運作，大幅提升我們的數據處理效率。
名詞「運作；操作」。片語 in operation 表示「運作中」。
After a successful operation to remove her appendix, the patient is now recovering well in the hospital.
在成功切除闌尾的手術後，病人目前在醫院恢復良好。
名詞「手術」。常用動詞搭配 undergo an operation 或 perform an operation。
The military launched a large-scale rescue operation to find the missing hikers in the remote mountainous area.
軍方發起了一項大規模的救援行動，以尋找偏遠山區失蹤的登山客。
名詞「行動；作業」。常用於軍事或救援情境。
Related: operate, operational

名詞「機會」。常用於指有利於達成某事的時機或情況。注意搭配介系詞 for 或 to V。常考片語：take the opportunity (把握機會), miss an opportunity (錯失機會)。
Studying abroad offers a unique opportunity for students to experience different cultures and improve their language skills.
出國留學為學生提供了體驗不同文化和提高語言技能的獨特機會。
名詞「機會」。常搭配介系詞 for + N 或 to + V。
He took the opportunity to ask the professor about his research project after the lecture, gaining valuable insights.
他利用講座結束後的機會向教授詢問他的研究計畫，獲得了寶貴的見解。
名詞「機會」。片語 take the opportunity to V 表示「把握機會做某事」。
Related: opportunities

形容詞「相反的；對面的」；名詞「相反的人/物」；介系詞「在...對面」；副詞「在對面」。詞性多變，需注意上下文。常考搭配：the opposite of (與...相反), on the opposite side (在對面)。
Despite their similar appearances, the twins have completely opposite personalities; one is outgoing, and the other is shy.
儘管外表相似，這對雙胞胎的個性卻完全相反；一個外向，另一個害羞。
形容詞「相反的」。
What he said was the exact opposite of what he truly believed, which confused everyone in the meeting.
他所說的與他真正相信的完全相反，這讓會議中的每個人都感到困惑。
名詞「相反的事物」。片語 the opposite of...。
The new coffee shop is located directly opposite the main entrance of the university library, making it convenient for students.
這家新咖啡店就位於大學圖書館正門的對面，對學生來說很方便。
介系詞「在...對面」。
She waved to her friend who was standing opposite on the other side of the street, waiting for the bus.
她向站在街對面等公車的朋友揮手。
副詞「在對面」。

形容詞「樂觀的」。指對未來抱持積極、正向的態度。常搭配介系詞 about。注意其名詞形 optimism。
Despite the difficult economic situation, the company's CEO remains optimistic about future growth and market recovery.
儘管經濟形勢困難，該公司的執行長對未來的成長和市場復甦仍保持樂觀。
形容詞「樂觀的」。常搭配介系詞 about。
Even after failing the first attempt, she stayed optimistic and believed she could pass the challenging exam with more preparation.
即使第一次嘗試失敗，她仍然保持樂觀，相信只要多加準備就能通過這個具挑戰性的考試。
形容詞「樂觀的」。
Related: optimism

形容詞「口頭的；口部的」；名詞「口試」。注意其名詞形 oral examination (口試)。
The teacher announced that the final English exam would include both a written test and an oral presentation to assess speaking skills.
老師宣布期末英文考試將包括筆試和口頭報告，以評估口語能力。
形容詞「口頭的」。常用搭配 oral presentation, oral tradition。
Maintaining good oral hygiene is crucial for preventing cavities and other dental problems throughout one's life.
保持良好的口腔衛生對於預防蛀牙和其他牙齒問題至關重要。
形容詞「口部的」。常用搭配 oral hygiene (口腔衛生)。
Many universities require international students to pass an oral as part of their admission process to ensure language proficiency.
許多大學要求國際學生通過口試作為入學程序的一部分，以確保語言能力。
名詞「口試」。通常指 oral examination 的簡稱。
Related: orally

形容詞「有機的；器官的；組織的」。主要指「有機的」（無化學肥料/農藥），也可用於描述生物體或系統的組成部分。
More consumers are choosing organic produce because they believe it is healthier and better for the environment.
越來越多的消費者選擇有機農產品，因為他們相信這對健康和環境更好。
形容詞「有機的」。指不使用化學肥料或農藥種植的食物。
The company's growth was an organic process, developing naturally from small beginnings into a large enterprise.
公司的成長是一個有機的過程，從微小的開端自然發展成一個大型企業。
形容詞「有機的；自然的」。指自然發展、非人為干預的。
The human body is a complex organic system, with each organ playing a vital role in maintaining health.
人體是一個複雜的有機系統，每個器官在維持健康方面都扮演著至關重要的角色。
形容詞「器官的；生物的」。指與生物體或其組成部分相關。
Related: organically, organism

動詞「組織；籌辦」。常用於活動、會議或團體。注意其名詞形為 organization，形容詞為 organized。
The student council decided to organize a charity concert to raise funds for underprivileged children in the community.
學生會決定籌辦一場慈善音樂會，為社區中弱勢兒童募款。
動詞「籌辦/組織」。常用於活動或事件。
The new manager worked hard to organize his team effectively, ensuring everyone understood their roles and responsibilities.
新經理努力有效地組織他的團隊，確保每個人都了解自己的角色和職責。
動詞「組織」。常用於組織團體或人員。
Before the exam, she spent hours trying to organize her notes and textbooks so she could study more efficiently.
考試前，她花了數小時整理筆記和課本，以便更有效率地學習。
動詞「整理」。指將物品或資訊安排得有條理。
Related: organized, organization

形容詞「最初的；獨創的」。名詞「原著；原作」。注意其副詞形 originally。
Her original idea for the science project was so innovative that it won first prize at the regional competition.
她對科學專案的獨創想法非常創新，因此在地區比賽中贏得了第一名。
形容詞「獨創的；新穎的」。強調獨特性和創造力。
Due to unexpected bad weather, the hiking club had to alter their original plan and choose an indoor activity instead.
由於突如其來的惡劣天氣，登山社不得不改變他們最初的計畫，轉而選擇室內活動。
形容詞「最初的；原先的」。指事情或計畫的起始狀態。
The museum proudly displayed the original painting, which was a masterpiece created by a renowned artist centuries ago.
博物館自豪地展示了那幅原作，那是一幅由一位著名藝術家在幾個世紀前創作的傑作。
名詞「原著；原作」。指藝術品或文件最初的版本。
Related: originally, originality

形容詞「戶外的」。通常修飾名詞，如 outdoor activities, outdoor sports。不可與副詞 outdoors 混淆。
Many students enjoy various outdoor activities, such as hiking and cycling, to stay active and enjoy nature during weekends.
許多學生喜歡各種戶外活動，例如健行和騎自行車，以便在週末保持活躍並享受大自然。
形容詞「戶外的」。修飾名詞 activities。
The school decided to hold the graduation ceremony as an outdoor concert on the main field, hoping for good weather.
學校決定將畢業典禮以戶外音樂會的形式在主操場舉行，希望能有好天氣。
形容詞「戶外的」。修飾名詞 concert。

副詞「在戶外」。通常放在句尾，表示動作發生的地點。不可與形容詞 outdoor 混淆。
After finishing their homework, the children rushed to play outdoors in the park, enjoying the fresh air and sunshine.
孩子們寫完功課後，衝到公園裡在戶外玩耍，享受新鮮空氣和陽光。
副詞「在戶外」。表示動作發生的地點。
On a warm summer evening, many families prefer to eat dinner outdoors on their patio or at a picnic table.
在溫暖的夏日夜晚，許多家庭喜歡在他們的露臺或野餐桌上用餐。
副詞「在戶外」。表示動作發生的地點。

形容詞「外部的；外面的」。常用於描述物體的外部部分，如 outer layer, outer space。
The Earth's outer layer, known as the crust, is constantly moving, causing earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.
地球的外層，即地殼，不斷移動，導致地震和火山爆發。
形容詞「外部的；外面的」。修飾名詞 layer。
Despite his rough outer appearance, the old man was known for his kindness and generosity towards everyone in the village.
儘管他外表粗獷，這位老人卻以他對村裡每個人的善良和慷慨而聞名。
形容詞「外部的；外面的」。修飾名詞 appearance。
Related: outermost

名詞「大綱；輪廓」。動詞「概述；畫出輪廓」。
Before writing her essay, the student prepared a detailed outline to organize her thoughts and ensure a logical flow of ideas.
在寫論文之前，學生準備了一份詳細的大綱，以整理她的思緒並確保想法的邏輯流暢。
名詞「大綱」。指文章、演講等的骨架或主要內容。
During the presentation, the speaker clearly outlined the main points of his research, making it easy for the audience to follow.
在演講中，講者清楚地概述了他研究的重點，讓聽眾容易理解。
動詞「概述」。指簡要說明主要內容。
The artist quickly sketched the outline of the human figure, capturing its basic shape before adding more details.
藝術家迅速勾勒出人物的輪廓，在添加更多細節之前捕捉其基本形狀。
名詞「輪廓」。指物體的外形或邊緣。
Related: outlined, outlining

名詞「烤箱」。常用於烹飪食物。
She carefully placed the cake batter into the hot oven, hoping it would bake perfectly for the upcoming birthday party.
她小心翼翼地將蛋糕麵糊放入熱烤箱中，希望它能為即將到來的生日派對烤得完美。
名詞「烤箱」。常用於烘焙食物。
Before roasting the chicken, remember to preheat the oven to the specified temperature for about fifteen minutes.
在烤雞之前，記得將烤箱預熱到指定溫度約十五分鐘。
名詞「烤箱」。常用搭配 preheat the oven。

副詞「在海外；往海外」。形容詞「海外的」。
Many university students dream of studying overseas to experience different cultures and broaden their academic horizons.
許多大學生夢想著出國留學，以體驗不同的文化並拓寬他們的學術視野。
副詞「在海外；往海外」。表示地點或方向。
The company is planning to expand its business into the overseas market, hoping to reach more customers globally.
該公司正計劃將業務擴展到海外市場，希望能接觸到全球更多的客戶。
形容詞「海外的」。修飾名詞 market。
After graduation, he decided to travel overseas for a year, exploring various countries before starting his career.
畢業後，他決定出國旅行一年，在開始他的職業生涯之前探索各個國家。
副詞「在海外；往海外」。表示地點或方向。

動詞「欠（錢）；歸功於」。常用於金錢債務或感激之情。
He still owes his friend fifty dollars for the concert ticket, and he plans to pay him back next week.
他還欠朋友五十美元的演唱會門票錢，他打算下週還他。
動詞「欠錢」。指有金錢債務。
She believes she owes her academic success to her parents' constant encouragement and the excellent teachers she had.
她認為她的學術成功歸功於父母不斷的鼓勵和她遇到的優秀老師。
動詞「歸功於」。常用搭配 owe sth to sb/sth。
After realizing his mistake, he knew he owed his classmate an apology for his rude behavior during the group project.
意識到自己的錯誤後，他知道他應該為在小組專案中粗魯的行為向同學道歉。
動詞「應該給予（某物）；欠（人情）」。常用搭配 owe sb an apology/a favor。
Related: owing, owed

名詞「貓頭鷹」。夜行性鳥類。
At night, a silent owl was seen hunting for prey in the forest, its keen eyesight helping it spot mice in the dark.
夜晚，一隻無聲的貓頭鷹在森林裡捕食，牠敏銳的視力幫助牠在黑暗中發現老鼠。
名詞「貓頭鷹」。指一種夜行性鳥類。
The distinct sound of an owl hooting echoed through the quiet woods, signaling the arrival of late evening.
貓頭鷹獨特的叫聲迴盪在寂靜的樹林中，預示著深夜的到來。
名詞「貓頭鷹」。指貓頭鷹的叫聲 hooting。
